title: Help with a custom formula

posts.author: iElizabeth77 (Elizabeth Smeller)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,
I’m tracking 2 different dates (Planned AND Revised) within 1 board and I’m looking for a formula that will provide the below results.
-Look at both dates, if either date is in the past AND in the current quarter, then the result is ‘delayed’.
-Look at both dates, if either date is in the future AND in the current quarter, then the result is ‘on time’.
Thanks in advance!

posts.author: CharlotteK (Charlotte Kirkham)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Elizabeth! Happy to give it a shot!

It sounds like you might actually want to use an automation for this! You could set up something like the following:

Screen Shot 2023-01-11 at 3.35.22 PM1206×430 26.3 KB

In the above scenario, you would make dropdown column labels for each quarter and the status would be a column with “delayed” and “on time” status labels. You would also need to set up a similar one for when the status is “on time”.

What do you think about this?

posts.author: iElizabeth77 (Elizabeth Smeller)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you so much! I will try this and let you know if it works.

posts.author: CharlotteK (Charlotte Kirkham)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, please do! Happy to help out!

posts.author: iElizabeth77 (Elizabeth Smeller)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there, this would work if there was a way to auto-populate the quarter based on the date vs having to manually select the quarter from the dropdown. I was able to create a formula field to populate the quarter, but I can’t use the formula field in the automation recipe so this is where I’ve landed (see screenshots). The last 4 columns are all formula fields and then I’m using a chart to filter by quarter. Note, the first screenshot is only a subset of data.

Screen Shot 2023-01-12 at 10.27.18 AM732×605 40.7 KB
